items.title: Oil jumps over 6pct to two-week high after Trump says deal with Iran 'over'
items.summary: BENGALURU: Oil prices jumped more than six per cent on Wednesday, hitting a two-week high after US President Donald Trump said the memorandum of understanding to end the conflict with Iran was "over", renewing fears of disruptions to Middle East oil supplies. Brent crude futures were up US$4.57, or 6.16 per cent, to US$78.73 a barrel at 0948 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude climbed US$4.23, or 6.01 per cent, to US$74.67 a barrel. The US airstrikes were in response to Iranian attacks on three commercial vessels that were transiting the Strait of Hormuz, US Central Command said on Tuesday.

items.title: Home Ministry targets one-year processing for complete citizenship applications
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Home Ministry has set a standard one-year processing period for citizenship applications, including those under Article 15A of the Federal Constitution, provided all required documents are complete, the Dewan Rakyat was told. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said incomplete applications would naturally take longer to process. Shamsul said that while the ministry adopts a balanced and comprehensive approach to every application, especially those involving children under Article 15A, national security, sovereignty and the country's interests remain the absolute priority.

items.title: High Court greenlights contempt case against Negeri Sembilan Undang over appointment dispute
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 8 — The High Court here has allowed contempt proceedings to move forward against Negeri Sembilan’s territorial chieftains and two other officials for allegedly defying a court injunction. Judge Roz Mawar Rozain granted leave to the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) and its secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, to initiate the committal proceedings. The action targets the territorial chieftains, known as Undang: Datuk Maarof Mat Rashid (Jelebu), Datuk Muhammed Abdullah (Johol), and Datuk Abd Rahim Yasin (Rembau).

items.title: Political parties should aim for enduring governance, not tactical alliances
items.summary: Few political parties have demonstrated greater tactical adaptability than Pas. This pattern has enabled PAS to survive periods that might otherwise have weakened many political parties. Can a political strategy centred on successive alliances continue indefinitely, or does every political formula eventually reach its natural limit?

items.title: Tengku Zafrul tells court PM’s minutes are ‘instructions’, says he usually acted on all minutes issued by then‑PM Muhyiddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Former finance minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz today said he had considered Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s “minutes” or handwritten notes as “instructions”, as the latter was the prime minister at that time. Testifying as the 11th prosecution witness in Muhyiddin’s power abuse and money laundering trial, Tengku Zafrul said he had usually acted on all of Muhyiddin’s minutes, including the latter’s minutes regarding government projects under the Bumiputera contractors’ programme Jana Wibawa. But Tengku Zafrul also clarified that Muhyiddin’s minutes or instructions were for the Finance Ministry to consider the companies for the projects, and not instructions to give the projects to these contractors.

items.title: Johoreans working in KL, Singapore eager to return for state election
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor natives working in other states, including Singapore, have expressed their excitement to return home and cast their ballots in the state polls this Saturday. Marketing manager Syed Abdul Rauf Syed Mustaffa, 34, said he will be taking leave to return to his family home in Bandar Baru Uda on Friday. Every time an election comes around, I take extended leave so I can vote and spend time with my parents," said Syed Abdul Rauf, who will be voting in the Larkin state constituency.

items.title: Japan corporate bankruptcies surpass 5,000 for first time in 12 years
items.summary: TOKYO: Corporate bankruptcies in Japan involving liabilities of at least 10 million yen (around US$61,600) climbed 7.1 per cent in the first half of 2026 from a year earlier, reaching 5,346 cases and surpassing 5,000 for the first time in 12 years, a credit research company survey showed Wednesday, reported Xinhua. Bankruptcies linked to price increases surged 27.6 per cent to 439 from the same period a year earlier, while failures related to labour shortages jumped 37.7 per cent to 237. June alone saw bankruptcies surge 20.4 per cent year on year to 1,021, the first time the monthly figure topped 1,000 in 25 months.

items.title: Cabinet approves another decade of tax exemption for TAR UMT
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The cabinet has agreed to extend the tax exemption approval for Tunku Abdul Rahman University of Management and Technology for another 10 years. Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the decision reflected the government's appreciation of TAR UMT's role in providing quality and affordable private higher education. He added that the university would remain subject to compliance reviews throughout the approval period, including the requirement to submit audited financial statements annually in accordance with Subsection 44(6) of the Income Tax Act 1967.

items.title: Singapore's Temasek reports higher net portfolio value of S$518bil
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore state-owned investment company Temasek Holdings reported a higher net portfolio value (NPV) of S$518 billion (S$1=RM3.15) as at March 31, 2026, up by S$49 billion against the preceding financial year. The group said the increase in NPV was driven by strong performance of listed Singapore-based Temasek Portfolio Companies (TPCs) and realised gains from key divestments. Temasek said its long-term returns remained resilient, with a 20-year total shareholder return (TSR) at 6.8 per cent and a 10-year TSR at 7.1 per cent, demonstrating the portfolio's ability to perform through market cycles.

items.title: Health Ministry forms special committee to probe Taiping Prison riot allegations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A special committee has been established by the Health Ministry to investigate allegations of misconduct involving its medical officers in connection with the Taiping Prison riot in January last year. "The ministry takes seriously the allegations involving the alleged alteration of medical reports by medical officers. Meanwhile, Hanifah said the ministry was strengthening healthcare service delivery in prison institutions through continuous engagement with the Prisons Department.

items.title: Perkeso's Lindung 24 Hours scheme now voluntary
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The cabinet has decided that contributions for round-the-clock social security coverage will no longer be mandatory, but voluntary. Government spokesman and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the decision on the Non-Employment Injury Scheme, or Lindung 24 Hours, was made after Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim raised public feedback on the matter. Perkeso said an average of nearly 20 cases a day were recorded under Lindung 24 Hours, which provides social security protection outside working hours.

items.title: Mental health screening to continue in schools, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mental health screening will continue alongside other initiatives under the "Sekolah Selamat" (Safe School) programme. "The reporting initiative and measures to address student disciplinary issues remain a ministry priority, while these efforts are further strengthened through initiatives such as the Generasi Madani character development programme. "Mental health screening is also being implemented and continued alongside other efforts.

items.title: [UPDATED] No change: Retirement age remains at 60
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has decided there is no need to raise the retirement age. Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cabinet decided today that the retirement age would remain at 60. In 2025, then Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said there was a need to raise the retirement age to 65 in both the private and public sectors.

items.title: Illegal workers' hostel, unlicensed hotel uncovered in Penang operation
items.summary: The discoveries were made during Op Sarang Burung, conducted simultaneously across four zones in the state's five districts by the Fire and Rescue Department, police, Penang Island City Council (MBPP) and Labour Department. Penang Fire and Rescue Department Fire Safety Division assistant director Alimaddia Bukri said the operation checked eight premises and issued 63 Fire Hazard Elimination Notices for offences under Section 8 of the Fire Services Act 1988. He said the offences included blocked emergency exits and staircases, faulty fire safety equipment, expired fire extinguishers, inadequate fire protection installations such as emergency lighting and exit signs, and the absence of fire-rated doors.

items.title: PM says federal-state cooperation vital to implementing national climate policies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The government will continue to strengthen the country’s climate change agenda, in line with Malaysia’s commitment under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that, for that purpose, the Federal Government needs to work closely with state governments to ensure that every climate change-related policy and strategy formulated can be implemented inclusively and effectively. He added that he emphasised this when he chaired the National Climate Change Action Council Meeting (MTPIN) today, which reviewed progress in implementing initiatives to strengthen Malaysia’s efforts to address climate-related challenges.

items.title: Morning showers forecast in six Johor districts on polling day
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Six districts in Johor are expected to experience morning showers on polling day for the state election this Saturday, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). MetMalaysia forecast rain in Johor Baru, Muar, Batu Pahat, Pontian, Tangkak and Kulai in the morning, while Segamat, Kluang, Kota Tinggi and Mersing are expected to enjoy fair weather. Overall, the 16th Johor state election will see 172 candidates contesting 56 state seats.

items.title: Malaysia stops military procurement from Norway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will no longer make military purchases from Norway following the country's cancellation of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) export licence, the government has decided. Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the decision was made at today's cabinet meeting. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Late buying lifts Bursa Malaysia to close slightly higher
items.summary: Among active stocks, both Zetrix AI and Pentech eased half a sen to 75 sen and 27.5 sen, respectively. AirAsia X shed four sen to RM1.14, Nationgate lost 3.5 sen to 80 sen, while GIIB inched up half a sen to 50.5 sen. Among the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 60 sen to RM34.34, Allianz Malaysia gained 38 sen to RM20.98, Nestle climbed 30 sen to RM91.10, Petronas Chemicals advanced 22 sen to RM4.47, while Ayer Holdings was 15 sen higher at RM7.35.

items.title: No graves to be exhumed for Penang's Mount Erskine underpass
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Island City Council said the the proposed Mount Erskine underpass will not affect nearby burial grounds. The Mount Erskine underpass project, aimed at improving traffic flow to Jalan Burma, was one of 10 projects the Penang government had sought funding from the federal government last year. The proposed 700m underpass will connect Jalan Mount Erskine to Jalan Burma beneath the busy Gottlieb-Bagan Jermal junction, a key traffic bottleneck serving Tanjung Tokong and Tanjung Bunga.

items.title: Social Welfare Dept reminds public not to share content that reveals children’s identities online
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 8 — The Social Welfare Department (JKM)has urged all parties to refrain from disseminating content that may expose the identity of children on social media and digital platforms. In a statement today, JKM said it took a serious view of the publication and circulation of photographs, videos and information that could expose the identity of children on social media and digital communication platforms, including content linked to a recent viral incident involving students at a school. “The public, especially social media users and media practitioners, are reminded not to publish, share or disseminate any information that could reveal the identity of a child involved in a case, whether the child is a victim, witness or is involved, or suspected of being involved, in a criminal act.

items.title: Road rage victim hopes prison changes meat cleaver attacker
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: After weeks of pain, trauma and surgery following a meat cleaver attack on the Federal Highway, Abu Mishary Mohd Fairus had only one hope for the man who slashed him — that prison would lead him to repentance. Abu Mishary's remarks came after water filter salesman Azmi Jantan, 43, was sentenced to 10 years' jail and two strokes of the rotan by the Shah Alam Sessions Court for the road rage attack last month. In a Facebook post, Abu Mishary, 45, said the sentence was not revenge, but a reminder of repentance and accountability.

items.title: Thai beer dynasty mother drops 'ungrateful child' case against son
items.summary: BANGKOK: A mother from one of Thailand's wealthiest families formally withdrew her "ungrateful child" lawsuit against her son on Wednesday, a Bangkok court said, after he accused his elder brother of sexual abuse. Jeeranuch Bhirombhakdi, a third-generation member of the billionaire family behind Thailand's Singha beer empire, dropped her suit against her son, Siranudh "Psi" Scott, according to a statement from the civil court that was hearing the case. She brought the suit in February under a measure known as the "ungrateful child law."

items.title: Court strikes out three ex‑assemblymen’s bid to challenge Perlis Speaker’s vacancy ruling
items.summary: KANGAR, July 8 — The Kangar High Court today struck out a writ of summons filed by three former Perlis assemblymen, who had sought to challenge the Speaker’s decision to declare their seats vacant. Justice Datuk Mohamad Abazafree Mohd Abbas, in his ruling, allowed Speaker Rus’sele Eizan’s application to dismiss the legal action filed by Mohd Ridzuan Hashim (Guar Sanji), Fakhrul Anwar Ismail (Bintong), and Saad Seman (Chuping) in January. “The defendant’s decision as Speaker is clearly connected to essential matters and falls within the parameters of the Federal Constitution and the Perlis State Constitution.

items.title: 24-hour notice issued to vacate Undang Luak Rembau's residence
items.summary: REMBAU: The Rembau district office has issued a 24-hour eviction notice for the official residence and Balai Undang of the Undang Luak Rembau to be vacated. The notice follows the proclamation of Hassan Ab Hamid as the 22nd Undang Luak Rembau at Istana Seri Menanti in Kuala Pilah on June 27. He said the balai serves as the administrative centre of the Luak Rembau customary institution, housing the Office of the Undang Luak Rembau and the offices of the Dato'-Dato' Lembaga of Luak Rembau.

items.title: French lawmakers back police shootings law dubbed 'licence to kill' by critics
items.summary: PARIS: French lawmakers have approved a proposed law that would give police officers involved in shootings the benefit of the doubt, which critics have dubbed a "licence to kill." Proponents of the law, including interior minister Laurent Nunez, say it is an important measure to protect police officers who work in dangerous conditions. "This law grants police impunity."

items.title: Aiman Athirah: Most local authorities miss 1pc PwD employment target
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Most local authorities nationwide have yet to achieve the target of allocating at least one per cent of jobs to persons with disabilities (PwD), the government said today. "The policy of allocating one per cent of employment opportunities in the public service to persons with disabilities was introduced by the Public Service Department (PSD) through Service Circular No. Aiman Athirah said facilities in some local authorities still lack dedicated lifts, ramps and disabled-friendly toilets.

items.title: Petronas received whistleblower complaint six months after alleged data leak to Petros, court told
items.summary: Petronas Senior Manager for Complaint Management, Laila Badriah Razali, 45, testified that she received the complaint via the Petronas Whistleblowing (WB) email channel from the complainant’s personal email address at 10 am on Dec 6, 2024. “According to the complaint, Mohd Khairul Akmal had sent confidential information relating to Petronas’ Upstream Business performance for the first quarter of 2024 from his personal email account to Petros. “The information was allegedly sent to two email addresses on June 8, 2024.

items.title: Police probe alleged primary school bullying after siblings kicked by schoolmate in Kota Kinabalu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Police are investigating allegations that two siblings were bullied and physically assaulted by a male schoolmate at a primary school in Kota Kinabalu, Sinar Harian reported today. According to the Malay daily, the children’s father lodged a police report yesterday after claiming his two children had been repeatedly harassed and assaulted by the pupil. Acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Abdul Rahman confirmed that police had received the report and investigations were underway.

items.title: Muhyiddin's minutes were 'instructions to act', Tengku Zafrul tells court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former finance minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz told the High Court that minutes issued by then prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin were instructions to act, not directives to approve projects. Tengku Zafrul said this during re-examination by lead prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin in Muhyiddin's Jana Wibawa corruption trial, clarifying remarks he made during cross-examination a day earlier. Asked by Wan Shaharuddin to explain his position, Tengku Zafrul said the context of each minute had to be considered.

items.title: COMPASS @ Kota Seri Langat fully taken up with 210MW data centre deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: COMPASS IP Sdn Bhd has signed a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) for a 14.6 hectare (ha) industrial plot at COMPASS @ Kota Seri Langat in Selangor, paving the way for the development of a large-scale data centre. The purchaser, Global Data Infrastructure Sdn Bhd, is part of a global infrastructure development consortium specialising in data centre projects. COMPASS IP director Datuk Stewart LaBrooy said Malaysia's strategic location, political stability, digital infrastructure and reliable power supply have strengthened its appeal as a destination for hyperscale data centre investments.

items.title: DBKL to acquire privately owned land to address Taman Datuk Senu access road closure, says mayor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is undertaking the formal process of acquiring land to resolve the issue of a blocked public access road built on privately owned land at Jalan Taman Datuk Senu, Sentul. Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said checks found that no land acquisition had ever been carried out in the area, resulting in the road, which has long been used by the public, actually being located on private land. Fadlun said the timeframe for resolving the matter would depend on the smooth implementation of the legal process at the JKPTG level, but DBKL remained committed to expediting the process.

items.title: Johor polls: Asyraf claims surge in Chinese, Indian support for BN
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: BN today claimed support from non-Malay voters, particularly the Chinese and Indian communities, has strengthened as campaigning for the Johor election enters its final days. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the trend was based on his observations after campaigning across nearly two-thirds of Johor's 56 state constituencies. "We are seeing encouraging momentum, especially among Chinese and Indian voters.

items.title: DBKL unveils RM45m upgrade for Jalan Kuari crematorium to improve services for non‑Muslim community
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is set to upgrade its crematorium complex along Jalan Kuari, Cheras to cut waiting times for grieving families and enhance the standard of end-of-life services for the non-Muslim community. In a statement today, DBKL said the RM45 million project, approved under the Thirteenth Malaysia Plan, First Rolling Plan for 2026, signals a long-overdue investment in dignified, accessible funeral care. And so must DBKL’s service standards.

items.title: Only 37pct of Orang Asli villages gazetted
items.summary: "At present, Orang Asli land is recognised through gazettement under the Aboriginal Peoples Act and the National Land Code. "A further 68 Orang Asli villages are currently undergoing land surveys by licensed surveyors for this purpose," she said. She said the Orang Asli Development Department (Jakoa) continues to engage state authorities to expedite the process.

items.title: Banks restructure RM4.7bil loans for 1,100 borrowers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian banks have processed loan rescheduling and restructuring applications involving more than RM4.7 billion in financing for over 1,100 borrowers since the end of April 2026, the Finance Ministry said. In a statement today, the government welcomed a series of initiatives introduced by the banking industry to support individual customers and micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said banks play a key role in helping Malaysians and MSMEs cope with rising costs and cash flow pressures through responsible and timely financial support.

items.title: Trump says interim accord to end war is 'over' after Iranian strikes
items.summary: ANKARA: US President Donald Trump said an interim agreement to end the war with Iran was "over" on Wednesday after Teheran carried out new attacks on US bases in the Gulf. In a flare-up of hostilities that pushed oil prices up to a two-week high, Iran said it had targeted US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after US forces struck Iranian targets in response to attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. The attacks further undermined efforts to turn the interim ceasefire agreement that was reached last month into a permanent peace deal to end the war, which began with US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran on Feb 28.

items.title: Civil society groups urge Johor, Negeri Sembilan voters to reject political apathy, turn out for state polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — A coalition of civil society groups today urged voters in Johor and Negri Sembilan to reject political apathy and cast their ballots in the upcoming state elections, warning that staying home would only undermine efforts to reform the country’s institutions. Speaking on behalf of the coalition, Kuala Lumpur and Selangor Chinese Assembly Hall (KLSCAH) president Ngan Teng Ye said the July 11 Johor and August 1 Negri Sembilan state polls would serve as an important test of public confidence in Malaysia’s democratic reforms. “Over the past few years, our country has witnessed an increasingly fragmented political landscape, while institutional reforms have progressed slowly, with some even stalling altogether,” he said at a joint press conference here.

items.title: Maritime expert urges MACC to expedite probe into alleged petroleum cargo discrepancies at Johor port
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 8 — A maritime industry expert Datuk Seri R. Jeyenderan today urged the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to expedite its investigation into allegations of discrepancies between bills of lading (BL) and the physical movement of petroleum cargo at Tanjung Langsat Port in Johor. He said the transactions, allegedly worth millions of ringgit, could have implications for national security and result in losses to the government. Jeyenderan previously explained that one of the biggest risks faced by shipping agents arises when the physical movement of cargo no longer matches the accompanying documentation, with any mismatch between operational handling and paperwork potentially exposing all parties in the supply chain to significant risks.

items.title: Witness tells court whistleblower complaint reaches Petronas six months after alleged leak
items.summary: Petronas senior manager for complaint management, Laila Badriah Razali, 45, testified that she received the complaint through the Petronas whistleblowing (WB) email channel from the complainant's personal email account at 10am on Dec 6, 2024. "According to the complaint, Khairul had sent confidential information relating to Petronas' Upstream Business performance for the first quarter of 2024 from his personal email account to Petros. "The information was allegedly sent to two email addresses on June 8, 2024.

items.title: DBKL unveils RM45mil upgrade of Cheras crematorium
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) will upgrade its crematorium complex along Jalan Kuari, Cheras, to cut waiting times and improve end-of-life services for the non-Muslim community. In a statement today, DBKL said the RM45 million project was approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan, First Rolling Plan for 2026. And so must DBKL's service standards.

items.title: HLIB cuts banking sector to 'Neutral' as earnings momentum fades
items.summary: HLIB Research analyst Raymond Ng said the current sector valuations have largely priced in optimism surrounding capital management initiatives, with limited scope for further capital reduction beyond CIMB Group Holdings Bhd and Public Bank Bhd. Ng said the banking sector is entering the second half of 2026 on a softer footing after early-year optimism. Following the sector downgrade, HLIB Research lowered Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank), Public Bank Bhd, RHB Bank Bhd and Affin Bank Bhd to "Hold" while maintaining "Buy" calls on CIMB Group Holdings Bhd, AmBank Group and Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd.

items.title: Motorists cry foul over Penang highway ramp’s sudden 40km/h speed limit
items.summary: However, Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng defended the enforcement, saying motorists must reduce their speed when approaching the sharp bend. “It is a sharp curve, and motorists must reduce their speed. Dennis also said any review of the 40km/h speed limit would require environmental, traffic and engineering assessments.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: Govt sets aside RM1m to rejuvenate downtown Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The government has allocated a total of RM1 million for the Downtown Kuala Lumpur Grants Programme 2026 to support heritage preservation and the rejuvenation of the city centre. “Whenever I spend time in Kuala Lumpur, I am reminded that this part of the city tells two stories at once. That is the city and the Kuala Lumpur we will build together,” she said when launching the programme here today.

items.title: Johor polls: Umno sec-gen says support from Chinese and Indian voters for BN has been encouraging
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 8 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is confident that support from non-Malay voters, especially Chinese and Indian communities, has been encouraging throughout the Johor election campaign period, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. He said this can be seen by the Indian and Chinese voters’ positive reception towards BN candidates and leaders at events. Asyraf said BN will focus on expanding their house-to-house and personal engagement, including reaching out to fence-sitters and non-traditional party voters, in the last leg of the election campaign.

items.title: Salesman jailed 10 years, caned for causing grievous hurt to motorcyclist with cleaver on Federal Highway
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 8 — A salesman was sentenced to 10 years’ imprisonment and two strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today after he pleaded guilty to causing injuries to a motorcyclist on the Federal Highway last month. The accused was charged with voluntarily causing grievous hurt to the motorcyclist using a meat cleaver, a weapon capable of causing death, at KM14.6 of the motorcycle lane along the Federal Highway near Section 13 at about 8.19 am on June 11. The victim suffered serious injuries, including fractures to his shoulder, left arm, fingers and wrist, as well as injuries to his head and left arm.

items.title: Johor polls: Pakatan’s Muhd Najib vows to develop Bandar Universiti Pagoh into a new socio‑economic hub
items.summary: PAGOH, July 8 — Bandar Universiti Pagoh should be further developed with more sustainable urban facilities to transform it into a new engine of socio-economic growth, particularly for residents of the Bukit Pasir state constituency. To that end, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Muhd Najib Lep is committed to enhancing the education hub if elected in the upcoming Johor state election, saying it is crucial to ensure the economic benefits are felt directly by villagers and small and medium enterprises (SMEs). Muhd Najib, who is also secretary of the Pagoh division of Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah), said the full potential of Bandar Universiti Pagoh, which is home to four higher education institutions, including the Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia (UTHM) Pagoh campus and the Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) Pagoh campus, should be fully harnessed.

items.title: Over 75,000 crypto mining machines seized in nationwide raids since 2022, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — More than 75,000 cryptocurrency mining machines have been seized in over 3,000 raids nationwide between 2022 and May this year, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar said. He said 629 people were arrested during coordinated operations by the Royal Malaysia Police, Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), local authorities and other relevant agencies. Shamsul Anuar said the illegal activity continues due to strong demand for digital assets and the potential for significant profits from their price fluctuations.

items.title: Workshop owner pleads not guilty to causing grievous hurt to man with machete in Puchong
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 8 — A motor workshop owner pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of causing grievous hurt to a man by hacking him with a machete at a premises in Puchong last year. K. Devaidran, 42, entered the plea after the charge was read out in Tamil before Judge ‘Ainul Bashiirah Dona Don Biyajid. The court also set Aug 13 for mention.

items.title: Malaysia urges Islamic world to draw lessons from Gaza tragedy, shape future of humanity, says religious affairs minister
items.summary: The tragedy has also signalled for a renewal of the Islamic civilisation to one that is capable of contributing meaningfully to global peace and ethical leadership, he said, during a plenary session at the International Islamic Civilisation Forum in Tashkent, Uzbekistan. The Muslim world must look beyond its glorious past, Zulkifli said. “The question before us is therefore not whether Islamic civilisation once attained greatness.

items.title: Malaysia’s international arrivals rise 3.4pc to 17.5 million despite global challenges, says Tourism Malaysia
items.summary: Tourism Malaysia director-general Mohd Amirul Rizal Abdul Rahim said the growth was supported by visitors from Asean, China, Japan, Australia, Taiwan and Hong Kong, as well as several Central Asian countries, including Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan. “Nevertheless, the 3.4 per cent increase shows that Malaysia’s tourism industry remains resilient,” he told reporters after the signing of a Memorandum of Collaboration (MoC) between Tourism Malaysia and Tripadvisor here today. Under the two-year collaboration, which runs until the end of 2027, Tourism Malaysia will gain access to Tripadvisor’s performance data and travel insights to strengthen its data-driven tourism planning and marketing strategies, while also exploring co-branding campaigns, digital innovation and programmes recognising excellence in Malaysia’s tourism industry.

items.title: Malaysia aims to be leading regional maritime hub with Port Klang’s Third Terminal, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Malaysia aims to become a leading maritime hub in South-east Asia through the development of Port Klang’s Third Terminal on Carey Island, which is expected to take nearly 20 years to be fully completed, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said Port Klang currently has a handling capacity of about 15.14 million twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs), compared with Singapore’s Tuas Port, which is capable of handling up to 60 million TEUs annually. “Tuas Port is three times larger than the existing Port Klang.

items.title: Seremban police: Several major roads to close for post‑state polls simulation tomorrow and Friday
items.summary: He said among the main roads affected are Jalan Yam Tuan, Jalan Dato’ Sheikh Ahmad, Lorong Khalsa and Jalan Lim Kee Siong. “The affected junctions and roads to be closed or diverted are Jalan Tuanku Hassan/Jalan Yam Tuan (Madura), Jalan Lee Fong Yee/Jalan Yam Tuan, Jalan Lim Kee Siong/Jalan Jawa, and Lorong Khalsa/Lorong Jawa,” he said in a statement here today. Mohd Yatim said motorists are advised to use alternative routes, including Jalan Dato’ Abdul Malik, Jalan Tuanku Hassan, Jalan Tuanku Munawir and Jalan Dato’ Abdul Kadir.

items.title: Cloud‑based system speeds up patient flow at public clinics, 81pc seen within 60 minutes, says deputy health minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR July, 8 — The implementation of the Cloud-Based Clinical Management System (CCMS) at government health clinics has helped reduce patient waiting times, with 81 per cent of patients now able to see medical officers within 60 minutes. Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said the adoption of the Dental Information System (DIS) at dental clinics and the District Hospital Information System (DHIS) at hospitals had also improved service efficiency while helping to address long waiting times and congestion at public healthcare facilities. Hanifah Hajar said MOH would continue expanding healthcare digitalisation, with plans to implement CCMS at 2,917 health clinics and DIS at 728 dental clinics nationwide by 2028.

items.title: DPM Zahid: 12 million Malaysians have signed up for MyDigital ID as of June 30, with 114 govt services integrated
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the government is continuing to expand the use of MyDigital ID as a single sign-on mechanism for online government services through an integration strategy implemented at both the federal and state levels. He said the Chief Secretary to the Government issued a directive on Jan 14, 2025, requiring all ministries, federal departments, statutory bodies, state secretaries and local authorities to implement the single sign-on system using MyDigital ID. At the state government level, he said 19 online applications have so far been integrated with MyDigital ID, while another 28 applications remain under development.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan: LPT3 to be developed under public-private partnership model, bidder to bear full cost
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The proposed East Coast Expressway 3 (LPT3) project will be implemented through a public-private partnership (PPP) model, with the successful bidder expected to bear the full cost of development, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said today. However, he said the highway’s toll rates have yet to be finalised. He said the proposed 122km highway would be developed under a Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) concession model, although no concessionaire has been appointed.

items.title: Mohd Uzir leaves behind modernised, more integrated national statistics system, says DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 8 — The 36-year service of Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin in Malaysia’s statistics system came to an end with a lasting legacy, having strengthened the country’s statistical framework through the National Statistical System Strengthening (PSSN) agenda. The Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), in a statement today, said the agenda had driven several strategic reforms, including the establishment of the National Statistics and Data Council (MSDN) at state and district levels, the modernisation of the Statistics Act 1965 (Act 415) to meet digital-era needs, and capacity building in artificial intelligence (AI) and data science. “Mohd Uzir also pioneered the data democratisation agenda through OpenDOSM NextGen, expanding public access to official statistics through an open and user-friendly platform, while enhancing digital initiatives such as MyPAKW and MyLabourHub to ensure statistical services remain relevant,” DOSM said.

items.title: Johor polls: Malaysian border control agency activates maximum‑capacity operations at BSI, KSAB for returning voters
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 8 — The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) will operate at maximum capacity at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) to facilitate the return of voters from Singapore for the 16th Johor state election this Saturday (July 11). Its director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said the initiative includes opening dedicated lanes, activating hybrid counters and implementing contra-flow lanes to ensure smooth traffic flow and traveller movement at the country’s two main land entry points. “At BSI, the measures include opening dedicated lanes, with 38 inbound counters at the car zone, as well as the full operation of 35 e-gates, two quick response (QR) code counters and 18 manual counters throughout the period.

items.title: Johor polls: Over 300,000 voters expected to travel home, heavier traffic anticipated, says LLM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — More than 300,000 voters are expected to travel home to vote, leading to increased traffic on highways, especially in Johor, ahead of Saturday’s 16th Johor state election, said the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM). The public can check the latest traffic updates and highway incidents via LLM’s official Facebook page and TikTok account @llmtrafik, or contact the LLM Traffic Management Centre at 1-800-88-7752. Live highway traffic updates via CCTV cameras are also available on LLM’s official website, www.llm.gov.my, and the LLM Info Trafik WhatsApp channel.

items.title: LCS delivery schedule on track despite Norway’s missile export ban
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The Norwegian government’s cancellation of its export licence for the NSM (Naval Strike Missile) system to Malaysia will not affect the delivery schedule of the country’s littoral combat ships (LCS), Parliament was told. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin said in a written reply dated July 7 that the scheduled delivery of the ships is guaranteed by the Lumut Naval Shipyard (LUNAS). The Ministry of Defence has spent RM8.45 billion on the LCS project as of May 31 this year, representing 76.2 per cent of the overall project value, Parliament was told.

items.title: Future of finance hinges on human‑AI balance anchored in ethics, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes more embedded in finance, the future of the industry will need to combine human judgment with machine intelligence, guided by ethical leadership, accountability and a culture of responsible innovation. Minister of Finance II, Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, said this in his special address at the Asian Institute of Chartered Bankers (AICB) Nexus 2026 Conference, here today. Amir Hamzah said a resilient banking system depends not only on sound regulation, capital strength and technological capability, but also on people.
